text: Continuous integration, or CI, is a development practice where team members merge their code changes into a shared main branch frequently. Each merge triggers an automated build, which compiles the code into a runnable application. Following the build, a suite of automated tests runs to verify that the new code doesn't break existing functionality. The main purpose of CI is catching issues early, before they become bigger problems. All these steps are set up in a CI pipeline, which runs the process automatically whenever someone adds new code. This helps teams maintain high code quality and deliver stable software more quickly.
